source.title: Cortico-hippocampal Schemas Enable NMDAR-Independent Fear Conditioning in Rats

confidence_rationale: Finnie et al. show that NMDAR-independent learning (schema-enabled slot-filling) requires that the first-task memory remain hippocampus-dependent AND requires anterior cingulate cortex (ACC) memory maintenance. When ACC memory is disrupted, NMDAR-independence is abolished -- the schema (prior structure maintained in ACC) is causally necessary for rapid slot-filling on the second task. This is a causal demonstration that the prior structure (schema in cortico-hippocampal circuit) must be maintained before subsequent posterior inference (attribution on a new similar task) can proceed efficiently. The NMDAR-dependency shift is a molecular signature of prior-enabled vs. prior-absent learning. Mapping to INV-044 is moderately strong because it demonstrates causal prior-dependency, but the mechanism is cortico-hippocampal maintenance rather than an offline SWS-analog installation phase.

mapping.source_claim_statement: NMDAR-independent learning on a second conditioning task (schema-enabled slot-filling) requires: (1) that the first-task memory remain hippocampus-dependent (prior structure is in hippocampally-accessible form), and (2) that ACC maintains the first-task memory (cortical prior maintenance). Disrupting ACC memory maintenance reinstates NMDAR-dependency -- the prior is gone and slot-filling must start from scratch.
mapping.ree_translation: INV-044: This paper provides causal evidence that a pre-formed prior structure (first-task schema maintained in cortico-hippocampal circuit) is a prerequisite for efficient posterior inference (second-task slot-filling without NMDAR plasticity). When the prior is disrupted, posterior inference becomes prior-absent (degenerate prior) and requires full plasticity again. This maps directly onto INV-044's claim: prior construction must precede posterior inference; co-computing them in a single online pass prevents the prior from being available in a form that enables efficient slot-filling.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The paper demonstrates cortico-hippocampal prior maintenance rather than an offline SWS installation phase specifically. The prior here is maintained as a persistent memory trace, not explicitly installed during a sleep phase. The mapping to INV-044's temporal architecture (SWS installs prior, REM fills slots) requires an additional step: that the prior maintenance process is what SWS accomplishes. This is a plausible but indirect connection.
mapping.source_context: Rat contextual fear conditioning; bilateral hippocampal and ACC pharmacological inactivation; NMDAR antagonist injection; two-conditioning protocol. Current Biology 2018.

confidence_components.notes: Good source quality (Current Biology, causal manipulation, clear NMDAR-dependency criterion). Mapping fidelity moderate -- causal prior-dependency demonstrated but not the temporal SWS/sleep-phase installation specifically. Transfer risk moderate: fear conditioning in rats vs. REE causal attribution in V3 substrate.
